How to handle this? Stack seems like a common approach. When we encounter a '(', push the current counts onto the stack. When we see a ')', then look for the number following it, and multiply the counts inside the parentheses by that number, then merge them back into the previous counts.

So, maybe using a stack of maps. Each map represents the current counts of atoms at that level. For example, when we hit '(', push the current map into the stack and start a new one. Then when we hit ')', process the multiplier and merge the current map into the previous one.

private int parseNum(String formula, int i) {
    int start = i;
    while (i < formula.length() && Character.isDigit(formula.charAt(i))) {
        i++;
    }
    return Integer.parseInt(formula.substring(start, i));
}

HashMap<String, Integer> current = new HashMap<>();
stack.push(current);
int i = 0;
while (i < formula.length()) {
    char c = formula.charAt(i);
    if (c == '(') {
        stack.push(new HashMap<>());
        i++;
    } else if (c == ')') {
        // process the closing parenthesis
        i++; // move past the ')'
        int multiplier = 1;
        if (i < formula.length() && Character.isDigit(formula.charAt(i))) {
            // parse the number
            int start = i;
            while (i < formula.length() && Character.isDigit(formula.charAt(i))) {
                i++;
            }
            multiplier = Integer.parseInt(formula.substring(start, i));
        }
        // multiply current by multiplier
        HashMap<String, Integer> multiplied = new HashMap<>();
        for (Map.Entry<String, Integer> entry : current.entrySet()) {
            multiplied.put(entry.getKey(), entry.getValue() * multiplier);
        }
        // pop the previous map from stack
        HashMap<String, Integer> previous = stack.pop();
        // merge multiplied into previous
        for (Map.Entry<String, Integer> entry : multiplied.entrySet()) {
            String key = entry.getKey();
            previous.put(key, previous.getOrDefault(key, 0) + entry.getValue());
        }
        current = previous;
    } else if (Character.isUpperCase(c)) {
        // parse element name
        int start = i;
        i++;
        while (i < formula.length() && Character.isLowerCase(formula.charAt(i))) {
            i++;
        }
        String element = formula.substring(start, i);
        // parse number
        int num = 1;
        if (i < formula.length() && Character.isDigit(formula.charAt(i))) {
            int startNum = i;
            while (i < formula.length() && Character.isDigit(formula.charAt(i))) {
                i++;
            }
            num = Integer.parseInt(formula.substring(startNum, i));
        }
        current.put(element, current.getOrDefault(element, 0) + num);
    } else {
        // according to problem statement, input is valid, so this shouldn't happen
        i++;
    }
}

// After processing all characters, current contains the counts
TreeMap<String, Integer> sorted = new TreeMap<>(current);
StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
for (Map.Entry<String, Integer> entry : sorted.entrySet()) {
    sb.append(entry.getKey());
    if (entry.getValue() > 1) {
        sb.append(entry.getValue());
    }
}
return sb.toString();

Now, putting all this into code.

Now, let's code this in Java.

The starter code is:

class Solution {
    public String countOfAtoms(String formula) {
        
    }
}

So, inside the method, we need to implement the stack-based approach.

Now, code steps:

Initialize stack, current, etc.

But in Java, the code would be:

Stack<HashMap<String, Integer>> stack = new Stack<>();
HashMap<String, Integer> current = new HashMap<>();
stack.push(current);
int i = 0;
while (i < formula.length()) {
    char c = formula.charAt(i);
    if (c == '(') {
        stack.push(new HashMap<>());
        i++;
    } else if (c == ')') {
        // process the closing parenthesis
        i++; // move past the ')'
        int multiplier = 1;
        if (i < formula.length() && Character.isDigit(formula.charAt(i))) {
            // parse the number
            int start = i;
            while (i < formula.length() && Character.isDigit(formula.charAt(i))) {
                i++;
            }
            multiplier = Integer.parseInt(formula.substring(start, i));
        }
        // multiply current by multiplier
        HashMap<String, Integer> multiplied = new HashMap<>();
        for (Map.Entry<String, Integer> entry : current.entrySet()) {
            multiplied.put(entry.getKey(), entry.getValue() * multiplier);
        }
        // pop the previous map from stack
        HashMap<String, Integer> previous = stack.pop();
        // merge multiplied into previous
        for (Map.Entry<String, Integer> entry : multiplied.entrySet()) {
            String key = entry.getKey();
            previous.put(key, previous.getOrDefault(key, 0) + entry.getValue());
        }
        current = previous;
    } else if (Character.isUpperCase(c)) {
        // parse element name
        int start = i;
        i++;
        while (i < formula.length() && Character.isLowerCase(formula.charAt(i))) {
            i++;
        }
        String element = formula.substring(start, i);
        // parse number
        int num = 1;
        if (i < formula.length() && Character.isDigit(formula.charAt(i))) {
            int startNum = i;
            while (i < formula.length() && Character.isDigit(formula.charAt(i))) {
                i++;
            }
            num = Integer.parseInt(formula.substring(startNum, i));
        }
        current.put(element, current.getOrDefault(element, 0) + num);
    } else {
        // according to problem statement, input is valid, so this shouldn't happen
        i++;
    }
}

// After processing all characters, current contains the counts
TreeMap<String, Integer> sorted = new TreeMap<>(current);
StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
for (Map.Entry<String, Integer> entry : sorted.entrySet()) {
    sb.append(entry.getKey());
    if (entry.getValue() > 1) {
        sb.append(entry.getValue());
    }
}
return sb.toString();
